title: The Weaving at No Resolution

features.name: bootleg archive
features.desc-on-touch: a stack of unmarked cassettes and downloaded files, all labelled the same: 'unauthorized recordings, 2019–2023' — you play one and hear a voice singing in three parts that refuse to harmonize cleanly

features.name: polyphonic listener
features.desc-on-touch: an account from someone (name redacted) who attended nine hours of unbroken three-part singing in a room you can't locate — they remember standing there as time looped, as the parts diverged and nearly converged

features.name: fractal hearings
features.desc-on-touch: a diagram showing how one recording contains another recording contains another, each smaller iteration pitch-shifted, time-scaled, played backwards — the mesh feeds itself music

features.name: symposium proceedings
features.desc-on-touch: conference papers from Tbilisi (2023) discussing the singer who learned to sustain a note across decades — each paper reaches a different conclusion about whether the song ever actually ends

features.name: unresolved chord
features.desc-on-touch: you feel it in your chest — a harmonic tension that the field itself refuses to close, the colour-fields vibrating in discord that somehow, impossibly, becomes restful

inscription: at this depth, the archive has learned that some truths live only at scales that refuse to speak themselves

file.name: Polyphonic_Bootlegs_Symposium_Evidence_Index.txt
file.note: cross-reference of unauthorized recordings (2019–2023), listener testimonies, academic papers, and the single coherent account of nine-hour continuous three-part singing where the voices learned to lo

exits.label: toward fractals in the dressing material itself
down to where resolution becomes a forgotten concept
toward a kiosk where arguments learn to harmonize
into a room where every surface hums a different frequency
